name: kanbantic-claude-plugin
description: v2.13.0 (KBT-F470 — app-scoped Version-CRUD over all surfaces): two new Version slash-commands ship. `/kanbantic-version-create` is a non-destructive wrapper around the app-scoped `create_version` MCP tool (PR #242: workspaceId + applicationId + name + optional description) — it resolves the owning Application by slug and needs no confirmation. `/kanbantic-version-delete` wraps the new `delete_version` MCP tool and follows the mutating-confirm pattern of `/kanbantic-version-freeze`: it resolves the version-code, shows code/name/status AND the concrete associated-issue count, and requires an explicit `yes` — the prompt WARNS that the delete cascades and permanently deletes every Issue whose VersionId is this Version (KBT-BD158), with a no-op abort path that makes no MCP call. `known-mcp-tools.json` is re-synced: `delete_version` is added while the 4 deprecated release-tools stay excluded (known-mcp-tools.test.js). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.13.0.md. v2.12.0 (KBT-E066 — version-aware plugin track, F318/F319/F320): the release concept is renamed to Version end-to-end across the 7 lane/intake skills (with a 1-cycle backward-compat shim for the old `release` term), the lane-skills carry the live version-flow tools (mcp__kanbantic__ prefix restored), and known-mcp-tools.json is curated to the live registry — the 12 Version-flow tools are present and the 4 legacy release-tools (create_release/list_releases/update_release/get_release_notes) are excluded. lint-skills.js gains Invariant 5 (stale release-domain tokens in lane-skills fail CI unless opt-out-marked). Two zero-dependency Node hooks: a PreToolUse `pre-tool-use-locked-version-blocker` that blocks claim_issue when the issue’s delivered-in Version is locked-on-deploy (lifecycleStatus ≥ StagingDeployed, KBT-F458) — fail-open on any infra error — and a Stop `stop-version-summary`. The plugin self-releases as the first Application through the new Version-flow (Version v2.12.0 → prod-deploy → F6-handler auto-creates the GitHub Release, KBT-TC2368). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.12.0.md. Kanbantic issue lifecycle skills: intake (report / request / propose), triage, prepare, execute, review — all artifacts via Kanbantic MCP. v2.11.0 (KBT-E087 — Agent-orchestratie & modelbeleid) adds: (F436) a new /kanbantic-orchestrate sequencer skill that, given {workspace, initiative, repos}, selects and orders actionable issues and drives each through the lane-skills (triage → prepare → execute → review) — explicitly NOT a lane-skill, overridable per workspace via a Toolkit Skill item of the same slug; (F438) a launch-orchestrator.ps1/.sh script that resolves KANBANTIC_API_KEY (env → HKCU fallback), launches Claude Code with the channels flag, and fails fast without spawning when key/params are missing — full Workstation-Daemon SpawnCommand integration deferred until Agent Sessions (v0.14.0) matures (KBT-BD151); (F447) context-aware worktree/repo HARD-GATEs in the prepare/execute/review lane-skills, skipped in no-repo / MCP-only contexts (Cowork/desktop) via a pure gate-context.js helper while real code-in-repo work keeps the strict gate (KBT-BD155); and (F437, sync) sync-workspace-skills now emits a `model:` frontmatter line for Subagent items carrying the new Model field and folds the model into its drift hash. Companion Kanbantic API change (F437): a nullable SubagentModel field (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) on Toolkit Subagent items, honored at spawn. See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.11.0.md. v2.10.1 fixes a bug (KBT-B349) in the filePath proxy augmentation introduced in v2.10.0: `content` was not removed from `required` in the tools/list schema, causing Claude to provide both `content` and `filePath` simultaneously, which triggered the ambiguity guard and silently rejected every filePath call. Fix: augmentToolsListResponse now filters `content` out of `schema.required` when adding `filePath`. v2.10.0 adds a filePath parameter to the stdio proxy (KBT-F464): a tools/call may carry `filePath` instead of a large `content` string — the proxy reads the file locally (fs.readFileSync utf8), substitutes it into `content`, and drops `filePath` before forwarding, so large files (e.g. a 154KB HTML wireframe for add_wireframe_version) never enter the model context. Generic, not hardcoded: the substitution applies to any tools/call with a filePath argument, and the proxy enriches the tools/list response so every tool with a `content` property also advertises an optional `filePath` (never added to `required`). filePath + non-empty content → -32602 ambiguity error (not forwarded); unreadable path → -32603 with path + OS reason (not forwarded, no crash); content-only and neither-provided calls are forwarded unchanged (no breaking change). Trust-boundary documented in the README. Zero new dependencies; runtime startup guarded behind require.main===module so unit tests can require the pure helpers. See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.10.0.md. v2.9.0 enforces process rules A–E in lane-skills (KBT-F449 / KBT-E084): (1) prepare now declares a per-issue frozen test-policy Decision-entry before transitioning to Prepared — 5F.5 for Features, 5B.6 for Bugs — with N.v.t. requiring ≥20-char rationale and at least one level remaining Vereist; (2) a HARD-GATE in 5E.4 blocks create_test_case on Epics (Regel A: test cases belong to child Features); (3) execute Step 3c loads the frozen policy and enforces it as read-only, defaulting to Vereist/min=1 if missing; (4) execute Step 7 now checks Passed count ≥ min per Vereist level — zero test cases fails coverage even when no test case has status Failed (missing coverage = blocker, not just failing tests); (5) review Step 1b loads discussion entries to parse the frozen policy and count actual Passed per level; (6) reviewer-prompt item 5 always REJECTs for missing coverage on any Vereist level. KBT-TRUL013 is superseded and archived. 18 automated tests cover all paths. See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.9.0.md. v2.8.0 adds the /kanbantic-graduation skill (KBT-E085 / KBT-F431): graduates a concept from the Kanbantic Toolkit into a fully built, reviewed, and merged implementation by orchestrating the full triage → prepare → execute → review pipeline in one command. Seven-step workflow with a 5-criteria ripeness HARD-GATE (KBT-TRUL018) and KBT-RL123-formatted derived test cases. v2.7.0 ships a zero-dependency git credential helper (KBT-B330): the lane-skills (execute / review / prepare) no longer clone with the PAT embedded in the URL — which persisted the plaintext secret into .git/config — and instead configure `credential.helper` so git fetches the token just-in-time from Kanbantic's get_repository_credential over stdin. The PAT never lands in .git/config, a remote URL, shell history, the process list, or the agent transcript (the helper, not the agent, calls get_repository_credential). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.7.0.md. v2.6.0 adds a readiness-gate override governance flag in the stdio proxy (KBT-F341 / KBT-E069): a successful update_issue_status / claim_issue carrying an overrideReason now triggers a greppable [override-governance] Comment on the affected issue for second-party review. Transparent-bridge preserved (response forwarded verbatim; flag is a fire-and-forget side-effect); opt out with KANBANTIC_SKIP_OVERRIDE_FLAG=1. Companion server-side preventive controls tracked under KBT-E069. v2.5.1 narrows /kanbantic-sync-workspace-skills materialization to Skill+Subagent only (KBT-B250 / KBT-BD086): Command toolkit-items are reference-only snippets and intentionally stay Toolkit-only — v2.5.0 silently exposed them as slash-commands under .claude/commands/, polluting the namespace. v2.5.0 adds /kanbantic-sync-workspace-skills (KBT-F265 / KBT-TRUL014): materializes the active workspace's Toolkit Skill / Subagent items as on-disk `.claude/commands/*.md` and `.claude/agents/*.md` mirrors with a `.kanbantic-sync.json` manifest for drift detection. Companion to Kanbantic API change KBT-F264 (bootstrap_agent now also returns skills/commands/subagents/customs arrays). v2.4.2 patches lane-skill SKILL.md mechanical inconsistencies (KBT-B192 / KBT-RL064). v2.4.1 adds a real-proxy regression test + bundle ↔ live MCP-registry drift detector (KBT-B200). v2.4.0 introduces Phase-of-Features-of-Tasks Epic shape (KBT-F250). v2.3.0 introduced InDeployment (KBT-F236), v2.2.0 introduced Prepared (KBT-F235). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.6.0.md.
